title: Placus balsamifer
taxonomy: Placus balsamifer is classified within the plant family Celastraceae. This family comprises about 60-70 genera and more than 1,000 species of flowering plants. Celastraceae is a diverse group of plants that are primarily native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The family is often referred to as the staff tree or bittersweet family due to its characteristic bittersweet berries. The classification of Placus balsamifer within this family highlights the plant's unique characteristics and its place among other flowering plants.

Its resin, which contains a mixture of compounds, has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
